title: Ciliaria funstonii
taxonomy: Ciliaria funstonii belongs to the family Polygonaceae and is a species of flowering plant. It is native to the southwestern United States, specifically in the deserts of Arizona, California, and Nevada. The plant is an annual or perennial herb that produces small, white or pink flowers. Its classification also includes the genus Ciliaria, which is part of the broader taxonomic group of Polygonum. Ciliaria funstonii is further categorized as a species within the Polygonum genus.

intro:
There is no information available about the plant 'Ciliaria funstonii' however I've found information about the plant 'Clelia funstonii' or more commonly known as 'Funston's milkvetch' which is a plant that belongs to the Fabaceae family, a type of legume.

Clelia funstonii, or Funston's milkvetch, is a perennial plant that has been used in traditional medicine for its various health benefits. The plant is said to have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help in reducing pain and inflammation, as well as protecting the body from oxidative stress. The bioactive constituents of Funston's milkvetch include alkaloids, flavonoids, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The plant is commonly used to make herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to treat a range of health issues including arthritis, joint pain, and digestive problems.
